title: A Community Event
summary: A narrator describes a summer fair organised by local volunteers in her neighbourhood, highlighting the activities, food, and spirit of community that made the day special.
body: Last Saturday, our neighbourhood held its annual summer fair in the park at the end of our street. The event was organised by a group of volunteers who spent weeks preparing for it. They set up colourful stalls selling homemade cakes, fresh lemonade, and handmade crafts. There was also a second-hand book stall where people could buy novels and children's books for just fifty cents each. In the middle of the park, a local band played cheerful music while families spread out on blankets on the grass. Children took part in a treasure hunt, following clues hidden around the park. Some older residents joined in a quiz about local history, and the winner received a hamper full of local produce. A face-painting booth attracted long queues all afternoon. By the end of the day, the volunteers had raised over five hundred dollars for the community library. Everyone agreed it had been a wonderful afternoon. Events like this remind us that a strong community makes a neighbourhood a much better place to live.

annotations.meaningVi: hội chợ mùa hè thường niên (hàng năm)
annotations.exampleEn: The school holds an annual summer fair every June.

annotations.meaningVi: được tổ chức bởi các tình nguyện viên
annotations.exampleEn: The charity run was organised by volunteers from the local club.
Traders set up stalls along the riverbank every weekend.
She sells handmade crafts at local markets.
The children loved the treasure hunt at the birthday party.

annotations.meaningVi: theo dõi các manh mối / gợi ý
annotations.exampleEn: The players solved the mystery by following clues around the building.
Over fifty students took part in the spelling competition.

annotations.meaningVi: sản phẩm địa phương (thực phẩm trồng hoặc sản xuất tại địa phương)
annotations.exampleEn: The market sells local produce from nearby farms.

annotations.meaningVi: quyên góp được hơn năm trăm đô la
annotations.exampleEn: The school fair raised over a thousand dollars for new equipment.

annotations.meaningVi: một cộng đồng gắn kết / vững mạnh
annotations.exampleEn: Living in a strong community makes people feel safe and supported.

annotations.meaningVi: thu hút nhiều người xếp hàng dài
annotations.exampleEn: The new restaurant attracted long queues on its opening day.

grammarNotes.title: Past simple for completed events
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì quá khứ đơn (Past Simple) dùng để kể về các sự kiện đã hoàn thành trong quá khứ. Với động từ có quy tắc, ta thêm '-ed': 'organised', 'raised', 'played', 'agreed'. Với động từ bất quy tắc, dạng quá khứ thay đổi hoàn toàn: 'held' (hold), 'set up' (set up), 'spent' (spend), 'took part' (take part).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use Past Simple to describe completed actions in the past. Regular verbs add '-ed': 'organised', 'raised', 'played'. Irregular verbs change form: 'held' (hold), 'spent' (spend), 'took' (take). Both types appear frequently in storytelling about past events.

grammarNotes.title: Relative clauses with 'where' and 'who'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Mệnh đề quan hệ dùng 'who' để chỉ người ('volunteers who spent weeks preparing') và 'where' để chỉ nơi chốn ('a stall where people could buy books'). Mệnh đề quan hệ giúp cung cấp thêm thông tin về danh từ đứng trước mà không cần tạo thêm câu mới.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Relative clauses give extra information about a noun. Use 'who' for people: 'volunteers who spent weeks preparing'. Use 'where' for places: 'a stall where people could buy books'. They make sentences richer without needing to start a new sentence.

translationVi: Thứ Bảy tuần trước, khu phố của chúng tôi đã tổ chức hội chợ mùa hè thường niên trong công viên ở cuối phố. Sự kiện này được tổ chức bởi một nhóm tình nguyện viên đã dành nhiều tuần để chuẩn bị. Họ dựng lên những gian hàng đầy màu sắc bán bánh tự làm, nước chanh tươi và đồ thủ công. Cũng có một gian hàng sách cũ, nơi mọi người có thể mua tiểu thuyết và sách thiếu nhi chỉ với năm mươi xu mỗi cuốn. Ở giữa công viên, một ban nhạc địa phương biểu diễn những bản nhạc vui vẻ trong khi các gia đình trải chăn ngồi trên thảm cỏ. Trẻ em tham gia trò chơi tìm kho báu, theo dõi các manh mối được giấu khắp công viên. Một số cư dân lớn tuổi hơn tham gia một buổi đố vui về lịch sử địa phương, và người chiến thắng nhận được một giỏ quà đầy các sản phẩm địa phương. Gian vẽ mặt thu hút những hàng người xếp hàng dài suốt buổi chiều. Đến cuối ngày, các tình nguyện viên đã quyên góp được hơn năm trăm đô la cho thư viện cộng đồng. Mọi người đều đồng ý rằng đó là một buổi chiều tuyệt vời. Những sự kiện như thế này nhắc nhở chúng ta rằng một cộng đồng gắn kết làm cho khu phố trở thành một nơi sống tốt hơn nhiều.

questions.prompt: Where did the summer fair take place?
options: In the park at the end of the street
questions.explanation: The narrator says the fair was held 'in the park at the end of our street.'
questions.hint: The location is described at the very start.

questions.explanation: The narrator says 'The event was organised by a group of volunteers.'
questions.hint: These were unpaid people who gave their time freely.

questions.prompt: How long did the volunteers spend preparing for the fair?
questions.explanation: The narrator says the volunteers 'spent weeks preparing for it.'
questions.hint: More than a week is mentioned.

questions.prompt: How much did books cost at the second-hand book stall?
questions.explanation: The narrator says books were 'just fifty cents each.'
questions.hint: A very low price is given.

questions.prompt: What were families doing on the grass during the music?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'families spread out on blankets on the grass.'
questions.hint: They sat or lay on something they brought.

questions.prompt: What activity did the children take part in?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'Children took part in a treasure hunt.'
questions.hint: They followed clues hidden around the park.

questions.prompt: What topic was the quiz about?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'Some older residents joined in a quiz about local history.'
questions.hint: The quiz was about something specific to the area.

questions.prompt: What did the quiz winner receive?
options: A hamper full of local produce
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'the winner received a hamper full of local produce.'
questions.hint: It was a basket-like prize containing local food products.

questions.prompt: What attracted long queues all afternoon?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'A face-painting booth attracted long queues all afternoon.'
questions.hint: It was an activity where something was drawn on people's faces.

questions.prompt: How much money did the volunteers raise?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'the volunteers had raised over five hundred dollars.'
questions.hint: The amount was more than a specific number.

questions.prompt: What was the money raised used for?
questions.explanation: The narrator says the money was raised 'for the community library.'
questions.hint: It is a public building where people borrow books.

cloze.template: Last Saturday, our neighbourhood held its annual summer {{b1}} in the park at the end of our street. The event was organised by a group of {{b2}} who spent weeks preparing for it. They set up colourful stalls selling homemade cakes, fresh lemonade, and handmade {{b3}}. There was also a second-hand book stall where people could buy novels and children's books for just fifty {{b4}} each. In the middle of the park, a local band played cheerful music while families spread out on {{b5}} on the grass. Children took part in a treasure {{b6}}, following clues hidden around the park. Some older residents joined in a quiz about local {{b7}}, and the winner received a hamper full of local produce. A face-painting booth attracted long {{b8}} all afternoon. By the end of the day, the volunteers had {{b9}} over five hundred dollars for the community library. Everyone agreed it had been a wonderful afternoon. Events like this remind us that a strong community makes a neighbourhood a much better place to {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: 'fair' — the event is described as the annual summer fair.
'volunteers' — unpaid people who organised the event freely.
'crafts' — handmade crafts are objects made by hand, often decorative.
'cents' — the books cost fifty cents each, a very low price.
'blankets' — families spread out on blankets on the grass.
'hunt' — a treasure hunt involves following clues to find a prize.
'history' — the quiz was about local history.
'queues' — people waiting in a line; the booth attracted long queues.
'raised' — to raise money means to collect it for a cause.
'live' — a strong community makes the neighbourhood a better place to live.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Community events like this summer fair bring people together and raise money for local causes. Do you think it is the responsibility of residents to organise such events, or should local governments fund community activities instead? What are the advantages of each approach?
